blurb: Digital signing and encryption underpin trust throughout Apple platforms. All executable code must be signed with a certificate issued by Apple through its developer program; the XNU kernel enforces code signing at execution time via AMFI. Firmware, OS images, and boot components use RSA or ECDSA signatures verified during the boot chain. For user data, S/MIME support in Mail enables signed and encrypted email using standard PKI certificates. Apple enforces Certificate Transparency to detect misissued TLS certificates, and all App Store submissions are re-signed by Apple after notarization to provide an Apple-attested distribution chain.
